description.task: Implement binary search variations to find the first occurrence and last occurrence of a target in a sorted array with duplicates. Use two separate binary search passes.
instructions: firstOccurrence when match: high=mid-1; (keep searching left)
lastOccurrence when match: low=mid+1; (keep searching right)
arr={1,2,2,2,3,4,4,5}: first 2 is index 1, last 2 is index 3, count=3
4 appears at indices 5 and 6, count=2; 6 not found, count=0
description.hint: firstOccurrence match: high=mid-1; lastOccurrence match: low=mid+1;

title: Ceiling and Floor via Binary Search

description.task: Implement ceiling (smallest element >= target) and floor (largest element <= target) search using binary search on a sorted array. These are fundamental operations for range queries.
instructions: ceiling when arr[mid]>target: result=arr[mid]; high=mid-1; (search left for smaller ceiling)
floor when arr[mid]<target: result=arr[mid]; low=mid+1; (search right for larger floor)
-1 means not found (target out of range)
description.hint: ceiling: result=arr[mid]; high=mid-1; floor: result=arr[mid]; low=mid+1;
description.expectedOutput:
Ceiling of 6:  7
Ceiling of 7:  7
Ceiling of 31: -1
Floor of 6:    3
Floor of 7:    7
Floor of 0:    -1

description.task: Implement binary search in a rotated sorted array (e.g., {4,5,6,7,0,1,2} rotated from {0,1,2,4,5,6,7}). At each step, determine which half is sorted and decide which half contains the target.
instructions: rotated={4,5,6,7,0,1,2}: 0 is at index 4, 4 is at index 0, 3 not present
rotated2={5,1,3}: 3 is at index 2, 5 is at index 0
This approach ensures O(log n) time even for rotated arrays
